objective: Plan a non-streaming OpenAI-compatible chat client (with tool calls) + health probe in core::explore::client over the existing ureq/rustls stack, working against Ollama and llama.cpp with typed errors.
key_changes: New core/src/explore/client.rs: wire model (ChatRequest/ChatResponse/Message/ToolCall/Tool), ChatClient trait, OpenAiCompatClient, ClientError, health_probe, HealthError.
ChatClient trait is the T03 dependency point (never the concrete type); OpenAiCompatClient::new(&ExploreConfig) POSTs {base_url}/chat/completions via ureq rustls, no new HTTP/TLS dep.
Wire model covers messages (system/user/assistant/tool), tools with JSON-schema params (serde_json::Value), tool_choice, response tool_calls; non-streaming only.
Dialect normalization for Ollama vs llama.cpp lives solely in this module: tolerant handling of tool-call `arguments` (object vs JSON-string), uniform ToolCall shape to callers.
ClientError enum distinguishes Connection (D3 shutdown signal) from Protocol/HTTP, derived from ureq::Error variant (Transport vs Status).
health_probe GETs {base_url}/models; HealthError distinguishes Unreachable vs ModelMissing, each with an actionable message.
Wire client.rs into mod.rs via pub mod client + re-exports of the public surface.
Cargo.toml unchanged (preferred): serde_json::Value covers arbitrary tool schemas; no new dependency.
No persisted schema/config changes — all new types are in-memory transport types; task only reads ExploreConfig.
Tests (no HTTP mocks): serde round-trips, both provider fixtures deserialize to identical normalized ToolCall, unreachable URL -> Connection/Unreachable errors.
Verify with cargo test --release --locked and cargo clippy -- -D warnings; assert no 'fastcontext' string.
Additive, non-material change; no version bump, no regeneration, no consumer breakage.
